title: Cost of Debt: The Altman Z-score uses a single financial ratio to determine...
summary: Evaluate whether the Altman Z-score uses one or multiple ratios to predict bankruptcy.
prompt: The Altman Z-score uses a single financial ratio to determine a firm's likelihood of bankruptcy.
solution: The Altman Z-score is a composite score that uses multiple financial ratios to predict bankruptcy, including working capital, retained earnings, EBIT, total equity, and total liabilities, providing a more comprehensive assessment.
